# Project Catalog (160 projects)

---

## NousResearch/hermes-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 178,323
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
The self-improving AI agent that grows with you — persistent memory, auto-generated skills, 14 platforms, 6 execution backends

Hermes Agent is a self-improving AI agent designed to maintain persistent memory and evolve its capabilities through a built-in learning loop. It autonomously creates and refines skills from experience while using FTS5 session search and user modeling to recall information across sessions. The system supports a wide range of LLM providers and can be deployed on various infrastructures, from low-cost VPS to serverless backends. Users can interact with the agent via a dedicated terminal interface or through multiple messaging platforms including Telegram, Discord, and Slack.

Highlights:
- Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement from task experience
- Deployment across six backends including Docker, Modal, and Daytona
- Cross-platform continuity via Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, and Signal

---

## farion1231/cc-switch

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/farion1231/cc-switch
GitHub: https://github.com/farion1231/cc-switch
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 90,220
Cross-platform all-in-one manager for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Gemini CLI, and Hermes Agent

CC Switch is a cross-platform desktop application that provides a unified management interface for various AI coding agents and command-line tools. Built with the Tauri 2 framework, it allows users to configure and switch between different environments like Claude Code, Codex, and Hermes Agent from a single dashboard. The tool simplifies the setup process for multiple AI interfaces on Windows, macOS, and Linux systems. It also facilitates integration with various API relay services and model providers to streamline developer workflows.

Highlights:
- Unified manager for Claude Code, Codex, and Hermes Agent
- Cross-platform support for Windows, macOS, and Linux
- Built using the Tauri 2 desktop application framework

---

## nexu-io/open-design

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/nexu-io/open-design
GitHub: https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 57,927
Local-first open-source design/prototyping system with coding-agent CLI support including Hermes Agent

Open Design is a local-first, open-source design and prototyping system that serves as an alternative to proprietary AI design tools. It operates by converting design systems into a filesystem of skills and plugins that coding agents can read, write, and remix to generate artifacts. The platform supports the creation of web prototypes, motion graphics, pitch decks, and high-resolution images using real CSS and components. It integrates with various local CLIs and agents, including Hermes, to automate design workflows directly from a user's laptop.

Highlights:
- Generates HTML prototypes, MP4 motion graphics, and PPTX decks.
- Supports 21 coding agents including Hermes via MCP server.
- Local-first architecture with sandboxed iframe previews and filesystem-based skills.

---

## mem0ai/mem0

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/mem0ai/mem0
GitHub: https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 57,560
Universal memory layer for AI Agents — official Hermes Agent memory provider, available as managed cloud or Apache-2.0 self-hosted OSS

Mem0 is an intelligent memory layer that provides AI assistants and agents with the ability to remember user preferences and adapt over time. It utilizes a multi-signal retrieval system combining semantic, BM25 keyword, and entity matching to manage user, session, and agent states. The project offers flexible deployment options including a Python/npm library, a self-hosted server via Docker, and a managed cloud platform. It is designed for applications such as customer support chatbots, healthcare tracking, and autonomous systems.

Highlights:
- Multi-level memory for user, session, and agent state
- Hybrid retrieval using semantic, keyword, and entity matching
- Support for self-hosted, cloud, and library-based deployments

---

## colbymchenry/codegraph

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/colbymchenry/codegraph
GitHub: https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 38,834
Local pre-indexed code knowledge graph for Hermes Agent and other coding agents to reduce token usage and tool calls

CodeGraph is a local developer tool that provides a pre-indexed semantic knowledge graph for AI coding agents to improve codebase navigation. It works by mapping symbol relationships, call graphs, and code structures into a local index, allowing agents to query the graph directly instead of performing expensive file scans or grep operations. This approach reduces token consumption and tool calls while accelerating response times for architectural queries. The tool integrates via MCP with various agents including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and Cursor.

Highlights:
- Reduces tool calls by approximately 58% through pre-indexed symbol mapping
- Operates 100% locally with no external dependencies or Node.js required
- Supports Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible agents

---

## iOfficeAI/AionUi

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/iOfficeAI/AionUi
GitHub: https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 27,495
Local open-source cowork app for OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, and other agent CLIs

AionUi is an open-source desktop application that provides a unified graphical interface for interacting with local and CLI-based AI agents. It functions by auto-detecting existing agent tools like Hermes Agent and Claude Code while offering a built-in engine that requires zero configuration beyond an API key. The platform enables autonomous file operations, web searching, and 24/7 scheduled task automation through a cross-platform workspace. Users can also leverage specialized assistants for generating editable Office documents, including Morph PPTs, Word files, and Excel spreadsheets.

Highlights:
- Unified GUI for Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and 13+ CLI agents
- Built-in engine for file access, web search, and MCP tool integration
- Automated generation of editable Morph PPT, Word, and Excel documents

---

## volcengine/OpenViking

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/volcengine/OpenViking
GitHub: https://github.com/volcengine/OpenViking
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 25,079
Open-source context database for AI agents — official Hermes Agent memory provider using viking:// URIs and tiered L0/L1/L2 loading; 91% token reduction and 43-49% task-completion gain over baseline on LoCoMo10. AGPL-3.0.

OpenViking is an open-source context database designed to manage fragmented memories, resources, and skills for AI agents. It replaces traditional flat vector storage with a filesystem-based paradigm that organizes data into a structured directory for recursive retrieval. The system utilizes a tiered L0/L1/L2 loading architecture to provide on-demand context while reducing token consumption. It also features automatic session management to extract long-term memory and visualizes retrieval trajectories to improve system observability.

Highlights:
- Unified context management using a filesystem-based organizational paradigm
- Tiered L0/L1/L2 loading structure to minimize token usage
- Recursive directory retrieval combining semantic search with folder positioning

---

## supermemoryai/supermemory

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/supermemoryai/supermemory
GitHub: https://github.com/supermemoryai/supermemory
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 24,938
Memory engine and API — official Hermes Agent memory provider with sub-300ms recall sustained at 100B+ tokens/month, custom vector graph engine, context fencing, and byte-level dedupe with 100% prompt-cache discount.

Supermemory is a context and memory layer for AI agents that prevents information loss between conversations. It uses a custom vector graph engine to extract facts, maintain auto-updating user profiles, and handle knowledge contradictions or expirations. The system provides a unified API for RAG, file processing, and real-time connectors to platforms like GitHub and Google Drive. It is the official memory provider for Hermes Agent and supports various MCP-compatible clients including Cursor and Claude Desktop.

Highlights:
- Maintains persistent memory graphs across multiple AI conversations
- Automated user profiling with static facts and dynamic recent activity
- Hybrid search combining RAG knowledge bases with personalized context

---

## garrytan/gbrain

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/garrytan/gbrain
GitHub: https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 20,745
Garry's Opinionated OpenClaw/Hermes Agent Brain

GBrain is an opinionated memory and synthesis layer designed to provide AI agents with persistent context across people, companies, and ideas. It combines a self-wiring knowledge graph that extracts entity relationships without LLM calls with a synthesis layer that provides cited answers and gap analysis. The system runs a background daemon to ingest, enrich, and consolidate data from sources like meetings and emails. It integrates directly with Hermes and OpenClaw agents and supports MCP for tools like Claude Code.

Highlights:
- Self-wiring knowledge graph extracts typed entity relationships automatically
- Synthesis layer provides cited prose answers with explicit gap analysis
- Supports multi-user company brain mode with scoped data access

---

## vectorize-io/hindsight

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/vectorize-io/hindsight
GitHub: https://github.com/vectorize-io/hindsight
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 15,568
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Agent memory that learns — long-term retain/recall/reflect workflows

Hindsight is an agent memory system designed to help AI agents learn and evolve through long-term retention, recall, and reflection workflows. It moves beyond simple conversation history by using a three-step process to store information, search memories, and generate disposition-aware responses. The system can be integrated via a two-line LLM wrapper or a direct API, supporting metadata filtering for per-user memory isolation. It is compatible with major LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama, and can be deployed via Docker or as an embedded Python library.

Highlights:
- Implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent learning.
- Provides a two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory storage and retrieval.
- Supports multiple providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and local models.

---

## mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
GitHub: https://github.com/mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 13,766
754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, ATLAS, D3FEND & NIST AI RMF

Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills is an open-source library of 754 structured skills designed to provide AI agents with the domain knowledge of a senior security analyst. The project uses the agentskills.io standard, providing YAML-frontmatter and structured Markdown playbooks that guide agents through step-by-step execution, prerequisite checks, and result verification. Each skill is mapped across five major frameworks including MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, and the NIST AI RMF. It is compatible with over 26 AI platforms such as Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Hermes Agent.

Highlights:
- 754 structured skills across 26 distinct security domains
- Unified mapping to MITRE ATT&CK, ATLAS, D3FEND, and NIST frameworks
- Compatible with agentskills.io standard for sub-second skill discovery

Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 12,879
Hermes WebUI: The best way to use Hermes Agent from the web or from your phone!

Hermes WebUI is a lightweight browser interface for the Hermes Agent that provides a visual alternative to the command-line experience. It is built using Python and vanilla JavaScript without the need for external frameworks or build steps. The interface features a three-panel layout for managing sessions, chatting, and browsing workspace files. It integrates directly with an existing Hermes Agent setup to provide persistent memory and provider-agnostic model access.

Highlights:
- Full feature parity with the Hermes CLI experience
- Three-panel layout with workspace file browsing and inline preview
- Zero-build architecture using only Python and vanilla JavaScript

Hermes Desktop is a native GUI companion designed to simplify the installation, configuration, and management of the Hermes Agent AI assistant. It replaces manual CLI management by providing a guided setup process for local or remote backend connections and a visual workspace for daily interaction. Users can manage complex agent features like memory, toolsets, and scheduled tasks through an intuitive interface. The application also supports extensive integrations with various AI providers and messaging gateways.

Highlights:

Web dashboard for Hermes Agent — multi-platform AI chat, session management, scheduled jobs, usage analytics & channel configuration (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp)

Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive dashboard and desktop application designed to manage Hermes Agent operations through a graphical interface. It centralizes agent control by providing real-time chat streaming, multi-session management, and a unified configuration suite for eight messaging platforms including Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp. The system includes specialized modules for monitoring token usage and costs, scheduling cron jobs, and managing multi-agent group chats with context compression. Users can also browse remote files, execute commands via an integrated web terminal, and manage model providers through a profile-scoped authentication system.

Highlights:

## outsourc-e/hermes-workspace

Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 5,299
Native web workspace — chat, terminal, memory browser, skills manager, and inspector panel

Hermes Workspace is a native web-based command center designed to provide a unified graphical interface for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It integrates real-time chat, a cross-platform terminal, and a file browser with specialized tools for managing agent memory, skills, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. The platform features a 'Swarm Mode' that uses persistent tmux-backed workers to orchestrate multiple autonomous agents across role-based dispatch lanes. Users can monitor agent state, track costs, and manage complex missions through a centralized dashboard and Kanban task board.

Highlights:
- Unified interface for chat, terminal, memory editing, and skill management.
- Swarm Mode for orchestrating multiple persistent, role-based autonomous agents.
- Zero-fork compatibility with vanilla Hermes Agent and dashboard installations.

## builderz-labs/mission-control

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/builderz-labs/mission-control
GitHub: https://github.com/builderz-labs/mission-control
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 5,156
Self-hosted AI agent orchestration — dispatch tasks, run multi-agent workflows, monitor spend

Mission Control is an open-source dashboard designed to centralize the management and orchestration of AI agent fleets. It utilizes a self-hosted, SQLite-backed architecture to provide real-time telemetry and task dispatching without requiring external dependencies like Redis or Postgres. The platform supports multi-agent workflows with built-in quality gates and integrates with frameworks such as CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen. Users can manage agent skills, monitor costs, and implement role-based access control from a single interface.

Highlights:
- Self-hosted SQLite stack with zero external dependencies
- Multi-gateway support for CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen
- Built-in Aegis review system for task quality gates

## campfirein/byterover-cli

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/campfirein/byterover-cli
GitHub: https://github.com/campfirein/byterover-cli
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 4,814
Memory as a git repo (formerly Cipher) — official Hermes Agent memory provider with 5-tier retrieval (4 tiers non-LLM, sub-100ms), brv vc commit/branch/merge versioning over plain markdown context tree. Elastic License 2.0.

ByteRover CLI is a persistent memory layer for AI agents that organizes project knowledge into a version-controlled context tree. It utilizes a 5-tier retrieval system and a Git-like workflow to allow developers to commit, branch, and merge markdown-based memory across different sessions. The tool features an interactive REPL, support for over 20 LLM providers, and 24 built-in agent tools for code execution and file operations. It integrates with major AI coding environments like Cursor and Claude Code to provide shared, synchronized context for development teams.

Highlights:
- Git-like version control for branching and merging context trees
- Sub-100ms 5-tier retrieval system for long-term conversational memory
- Supports 20+ LLM providers and Model Context Protocol integration

## plastic-labs/honcho

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/plastic-labs/honcho
GitHub: https://github.com/plastic-labs/honcho
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 4,712
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Memory library for building stateful agents — the upstream library that the elkimek/honcho-self-hosted Hermes wrapper builds on.

Honcho is a memory infrastructure library designed to build stateful agents that track evolving relationships between users, agents, and projects over time. It functions by capturing messages and events in sessions, then asynchronously extracting conclusions and peer representations through a background reasoning loop. This approach allows developers to query natural-language insights or retrieve prompt-ready context for injection into LLM calls. The system supports hybrid search, document imports, and integrates with frameworks like MCP and Hermes.

Highlights:
- Extracts reasoning-based conclusions from conversations instead of just matching text chunks
- Models multi-peer perspectives to track what entities know about each other
- Provides FastAPI-based server for managed or self-hosted deployment options

---

## alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book
GitHub: https://github.com/alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 4,039
Hermes Agent practical guide — Orange Book series (Chinese language)

The Hermes Agent Orange Book is a practical guide designed to help users master the Hermes Agent open-source AI framework. It explains the framework's technical architecture, including its three-layer memory system, self-improving learning loop, and automatic skill evolution. The guide provides 17 chapters of content covering installation, customization, and real-world applications such as development automation and knowledge assistants.

Highlights:
- Detailed guides on memory systems and learning loops
- Step-by-step setup and multi-platform customization instructions
- Practical scenarios for dev automation and multi-agent systems

---

## NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 3,821
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Evolutionary self-improvement using DSPy + GEPA — optimizes skills, prompts, and code

Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is an automated optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes Agent without requiring GPU training. It utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and mutate skill files, tool descriptions, and system prompts based on reflective feedback. The system validates candidate variants through constraint gates, including size limits and full test suite passes, before proposing improvements via pull requests. It supports evaluation using both synthetic data and real session history from tools like Claude Code and Copilot.

Highlights:
- Optimizes agent skills and prompts using DSPy and GEPA engines
- Operates via API calls without requiring local GPU resources
- Enforces strict guardrails including size limits and 100% test pass rates

---

## 0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 3,679
Curated list of awesome skills, tools, integrations, and resources for Hermes Agent

This project is a curated directory of resources designed to expand the capabilities and workflows of the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It organizes community-contributed skills, tools, integrations, and documentation into categorized lists for easier discovery. Users can find everything from deployment utilities and messaging bridges to specialized domain applications and multi-agent orchestration tools. The repository also includes maturity tags to help developers distinguish between stable production tools and experimental proofs of concept.

Highlights:
- Curated list of community skills and plugins
- Categorized resources for deployment and integrations
- Maturity tags for assessing tool stability

---

## junhoyeo/tokscale

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/junhoyeo/tokscale
GitHub: https://github.com/junhoyeo/tokscale
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 3,478
CLI tool for tracking token usage from Claude Code, OpenClaw, Hermes, Codex, Gemini, and more

Tokscale is a monitoring tool designed to track token consumption and costs across various AI coding agents. It works by scanning local data directories and configuration files to aggregate usage statistics from multiple clients. The tool provides a native Rust TUI for visualization and a dashboard for analyzing daily summaries. It supports a wide array of integrations, including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and GitHub Copilot CLI.

Highlights:
- Native Rust TUI for high-performance usage visualization
- Tracks costs across multiple AI coding agents and clients
- Supports data aggregation from local databases and JSONL files

---

## agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires
GitHub: https://github.com/agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 2,500
TUI and web control surface for managing multiple coding agents including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Gemini, and others

Agent of Empires (AoE) is a session manager for AI coding agents that enables developers to run and monitor multiple agents simultaneously across different codebase branches. It uses tmux to persist agent sessions in the background, allowing them to remain active even if the terminal is closed or disconnected. Users can manage these sessions through a terminal UI (TUI), a web dashboard, or a mobile-friendly interface with support for Docker sandboxing and Git worktrees. The platform integrates with various agents including Hermes, Claude Code, and OpenCode, providing unified status detection and diff reviews.

Highlights:
- Manages parallel agent sessions using tmux for persistent background execution
- Supports Docker, Podman, and Apple Containers for isolated agent sandboxing
- Provides TUI, web dashboard, and mobile-first native state rendering

---

## Agents365-ai/drawio-skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Agents365-ai/drawio-skill
GitHub: https://github.com/Agents365-ai/drawio-skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 2,066
Generate draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions

The drawio-skill project is a tool that converts natural language descriptions and source code into professional .drawio diagrams. It functions by generating XML files and utilizing the native draw.io desktop CLI to export them into formats like PNG, SVG, and PDF. The skill includes specialized extractors to visualize codebase structures for Python, JavaScript, Go, and Rust, and features an automated self-check mechanism to fix layout issues like overlapping labels. It integrates with various AI agents including Claude Code, Cursor, and Hermes to provide an iterative feedback loop for diagram refinement.

Highlights:
- Converts natural language prompts into editable .drawio XML files
- Visualizes codebase structures for Python, JS, Go, and Rust
- Includes 10,000+ official cloud icons and 321 AI-specific logos

---

## dodo-reach/hermes-desktop

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/dodo-reach/hermes-desktop
GitHub: https://github.com/dodo-reach/hermes-desktop
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 1,803
A native Mac workspace for Hermes: real SSH, real terminal, real session data.

Hermes Desktop is a native macOS workbench designed to provide a focused interface for managing Hermes Agent via SSH. It operates by connecting directly to a remote host, ensuring the remote machine remains the single source of truth without requiring local mirrors or gateway APIs. Users can manage sessions, Kanban boards, files, and cron jobs through a unified GUI while maintaining access to a real terminal. The application supports multiple Hermes profiles and works across various environments, including VPS, Raspberry Pi, or local SSH connections.

Highlights:
- Connects directly to Hermes hosts over SSH
- Provides native views for Kanban, files, and skills
- Includes an embedded SSH terminal for direct shell access

## conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing
GitHub: https://github.com/conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 1,671
Audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI writing patterns

avoid-ai-writing is a portable agent skill designed to identify and eliminate common AI-generated writing patterns. It utilizes a structured audit process featuring a 109-entry word replacement table and 47 distinct pattern categories to replace "AI-isms" with plainer language. The tool supports multiple operational modes and voice profiles, integrating with agents like Hermes, Claude Code, and OpenClaw via a standardized SKILL.md format.

Highlights:
- Three modes: Rewrite, Detect, and targeted in-place Edit
- Two-pass detection to catch lingering AI patterns after editing
- Compatible with Hermes, Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Cursor

## AMAP-ML/SkillClaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AMAP-ML/SkillClaw
GitHub: https://github.com/AMAP-ML/SkillClaw
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 1,596
Let Skills Evolve Collectively with Agentic Evolver

SkillClaw is a skill management framework that enables AI agents to evolve their capabilities through real-world interactions. It functions by running a background evolution loop that automatically deduplicates, improves, and merges skills across different sessions and devices. The system creates a unified skill library that synchronizes experience between multiple agents and users, ensuring that improvements made by one instance are available to all others. It natively integrates with the Hermes Agent ecosystem and supports various other platforms including OpenClaw, QwenPaw, and OpenAI-compatible APIs.

Highlights:
- Automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops
- Unifies skill libraries across multiple agents, devices, and user teams
- Natively integrates with Hermes, OpenClaw, and OpenAI-compatible APIs

## NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 1,479
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Run Hermes as a managed employee in Paperclip company systems

This project is an adapter that integrates Hermes Agent into Paperclip company systems to enable AI agents to function as managed employees. It works by spawning the Hermes CLI in single-query mode, parsing raw stdout into structured transcript objects, and managing session state across heartbeats. The adapter converts ASCII formatting into GFM markdown and supports a wide range of inference providers and toolsets. This allows Hermes to handle assigned issues and respond to comments while maintaining persistent memory and skill integration.

Highlights:
- Supports 8 inference providers and 30+ native tools
- Parses raw output into structured GFM markdown and tool cards
- Integrates Paperclip-managed and Hermes-native skills via sync APIs

---

## NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 1,374
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Function calling examples and training data for Hermes LLM models

Hermes-Function-Calling provides a framework for the Hermes Pro LLM to execute function calling and structured data generation based on provided schemas. It utilizes a recursive loop to generate tool calls, execute them via Python scripts, and return the results to the model for final response generation. The project includes implementations for retrieving financial data via the yfinance library and supports JSON mode for validating outputs against Pydantic models. It employs the ChatML prompt format to ensure compatibility with OpenAI-style multi-turn dialogues.

Highlights:
- Executes function calls for stock prices and financial fundamentals.
- Supports JSON mode with Pydantic model validation.
- Uses ChatML format for structured multi-turn chat dialogues.

---

## jau123/MeiGen-AI-Design-MCP

GitHub: https://github.com/jau123/MeiGen-AI-Design-MCP
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 1,351
Supports GPT Image 2, Nanobanana & ComfyUI, with a 1,400+ prompt library, carefully crafted hooks and a multi-task orchestration system

MeiGen AI Design MCP is an open-source Model Context Protocol server that enables AI coding tools to perform professional image and video generation tasks. It connects to various backends including the MeiGen cloud, local ComfyUI instances, and OpenAI-compatible APIs to process visual requests directly within development environments. The system features a library of over 1,400 curated prompt templates and supports parallel sub-agent orchestration for batch processing. It is natively compatible with major MCP hosts like Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and Cursor.

Highlights:
- Supports 9 image and video models including Midjourney and Flux
- Integrates local ComfyUI or cloud-based OpenAI-compatible API backends
- Includes 1,446 curated prompt templates for style-aware generation

GitHub: https://github.com/numtide/llm-agents.nix
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 1,347
Nix packages for AI coding agents including Hermes

llm-agents.nix is a collection of Nix packages designed to simplify the deployment and management of AI-powered coding agents and development tools. The project provides a centralized repository that automatically updates daily, ensuring users have access to the latest versions of various agentic CLIs. By leveraging Nix, it allows developers to run diverse tools like Claude Code, Goose, and Gemini-cli instantly without manual installation or dependency conflicts. This infrastructure supports a wide range of open-source and proprietary agents, facilitating seamless integration into terminal-based development workflows.

Highlights:
- Provides Nix packages for numerous AI coding agents and CLIs
- Automated daily updates ensure access to the latest tool versions
- Supports diverse agents including Claude Code, Goose, and Gemini-cli

## liaohch3/claude-tap

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/liaohch3/claude-tap
GitHub: https://github.com/liaohch3/claude-tap
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 1,303
Local trace viewer for inspecting coding-agent API traffic from Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, OpenCode, and others

claude-tap is a local proxy and trace viewer designed to help developers inspect the API traffic of AI coding agents like Hermes Agent and Claude Code. It works by running agent CLIs through a local interceptor to capture and display system prompts, tool schemas, and streaming responses in a web-based interface. Users can analyze token usage, compare request diffs between conversation turns, and export traces as self-contained HTML files. The tool prioritizes privacy by keeping data local and automatically redacting common authentication headers.

Highlights:
- Inspects prompts, tool calls, and reconstructed streaming responses locally
- Supports Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini CLI
- Generates portable HTML artifacts for sharing and archiving traces

---

## NousResearch/atropos

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/atropos
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/atropos
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 1,253
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
RL training environments framework for tool-calling models

Atropos is an environment microservice framework designed to facilitate asynchronous reinforcement learning for large language models. It utilizes a trajectory API that allows environment services to send data and trainers to pull batches for optimization. The framework supports a wide range of interactive settings, including code execution, multimodal tasks, and multi-turn tool calling. This architecture enables the training of specialized models for domains such as financial prediction and complex function calling.

Highlights:
- Supports dataset, online, multimodal, and code execution environments
- Provides a trajectory API for data collection and distribution
- Enables RL training for multi-turn tool calling and RLAIF

---

Cross-platform skills library for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents

Wondel.ai Skills is a cross-platform library of agent capabilities designed for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible environments. It provides pre-configured skill collections based on established industry frameworks, such as Jobs to Be Done, Clean Code, and Lean Startup methodology. Users can install these skills via the Claude Code marketplace or a dedicated CLI to equip AI agents with specialized knowledge in software architecture, UX design, and product strategy. The project bridges the gap between raw LLM capabilities and structured professional methodologies through modular, installable plugins.

Highlights:
- Compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents
- Modular installation via marketplace or npx skills CLI
- Frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy

## greyhaven-ai/autocontext

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/greyhaven-ai/autocontext
GitHub: https://github.com/greyhaven-ai/autocontext
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 1,175
Recursive self-improving context harness — helps agents succeed on complex tasks

Autocontext is a recursive improvement harness that helps agents solve complex tasks by iterating against real evaluations. It employs a multi-role system—including a competitor, analyst, coach, architect, and curator—to test strategies and persist successful outcomes. This process generates structured traces, playbooks, and artifacts that allow subsequent agent runs to inherit accumulated knowledge. The tool integrates with various providers like Anthropic and OpenAI, and supports MCP for integration with clients like Claude Code.

Highlights:
- Recursive loops that distill successful strategies into reusable playbooks
- Multi-role coordination for strategy proposal, analysis, and curation
- Support for multiple LLM providers and MCP server integration

---

## NousResearch/autonovel

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/autonovel
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/autonovel
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 1,055
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Autonomous novel-writing pipeline generating 100k+ word manuscripts

Autonovel is an autonomous agent pipeline designed to transform a seed concept into a complete, publishable novel. It utilizes a multi-phase workflow that includes world-building, sequential drafting, and an adversarial revision loop involving dual-persona LLM reviewers. The system integrates mechanical slop detection and voice fingerprinting to ensure prose quality and narrative consistency across tens of thousands of words. Beyond text generation, the project automates typesetting in LaTeX, cover art creation via fal.ai, and multi-voice audiobook production using ElevenLabs.

Highlights:
- Autonomous four-phase pipeline from world-building to final export
- Adversarial revision loop with mechanical and LLM-based evaluation
- Automated typesetting, cover illustration, and multi-voice audiobook generation

---

## ksimback/hermes-ecosystem

GitHub: https://github.com/ksimback/hermes-ecosystem
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 939
Hermes Atlas — the community map of every tool, skill, and integration for Hermes Agent by Nous Research

Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub star counts and growth trends cached via Redis.

Highlights:
- Directory of 80+ security-reviewed Hermes Agent tools and integrations
- RAG chatbot providing grounded answers from 27 research files
- Live GitHub star tracking with Redis-cached sparklines and trends

## AxDSan/mnemosyne

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AxDSan/mnemosyne
GitHub: https://github.com/AxDSan/mnemosyne
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 881
The Zero-Dependency, Sub-Millisecond AI Memory System for Hermes Agents

Mnemosyne is a local, SQLite-backed memory system designed to provide persistent context for Hermes and other AI agents. It utilizes a single-file database architecture combining vector search and a temporal knowledge graph to manage working and long-term episodic memory. The system operates with zero external dependencies and achieves sub-millisecond retrieval speeds through binary vector optimization. It integrates natively with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and popular agent frameworks like Cursor, Claude Code, and OpenWebUI.

Highlights:
- Zero-dependency SQLite backend with sub-millisecond retrieval performance
- Supports MCP, Python SDK, and native plugins for major agent frameworks
- Features episodic compression and temporal triples for long-term knowledge management

---

## CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room
GitHub: https://github.com/CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 844
Control Room-first template for managing Hermes agents from one VPS agent to specialist teams and orchestrated workflows

The Hermes Agent Control Room is a template-based management framework designed to organize and govern Hermes agents from a single VPS. It functions as a sidecar control plane that provides a structured environment for documenting agent registries, runbooks, and security protocols. Users can scale from a single agent to a multi-agent system using a task bus pattern for delegation between an optional orchestrator and specialized agents. The repository includes bundled skills for VPS provisioning, automated backups, and security auditing to ensure consistent operations across an agentic team.

Highlights:
- Provides a sidecar control plane for agent documentation and governance.
- Scales from single agents to orchestrated multi-agent specialist teams.
- Includes task bus patterns for structured orchestrator-to-specialist delegation.

## xaspx/hermes-control-interface

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/xaspx/hermes-control-interface
GitHub: https://github.com/xaspx/hermes-control-interface
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 738
A self-hosted web dashboard for the Hermes AI agent stack. Provides a browser-based terminal, file explorer, session overview, cron management, system metrics, and an agent status panel — all behind a

Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to centralize the management and monitoring of the Hermes AI agent stack. It utilizes a Node.js and Express backend with WebSockets to provide real-time interaction with agents, files, and system processes. Users can oversee swarm pipelines via a specialized Kanban monitor, manage MCP servers, and track token usage analytics. The interface also includes built-in security features like role-based access control and a password-protected gateway.

Highlights:
- Real-time swarm monitoring with a three-panel Kanban pipeline
- Full operational control plane for MCP servers
- Comprehensive token analytics and cost projection tools

## stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm
GitHub: https://github.com/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 656
Lossless Context Management plugin for Hermes Agent — DAG-based context engine that never loses a message

Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard flat-summary compression with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that stores raw messages in a SQLite database while compacting older context into depth-aware summary nodes. This architecture allows the agent to maintain a bounded active context window while using specialized tools to retrieve exact historical details through drill-down queries. The system includes automated controls for sensitive data redaction and externalizes large payloads to ensure storage efficiency without sacrificing recoverability.

Highlights:
- Uses a DAG-based engine to compact history into hierarchical summaries
- Provides agent tools like lcm_grep and lcm_expand for detail recovery
- Persists raw conversation data in a local SQLite message store

---

## jazzyalex/agent-sessions

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/jazzyalex/agent-sessions
GitHub: https://github.com/jazzyalex/agent-sessions
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 604
Native macOS session browser, agent cockpit, analytics, and limits tracker for Hermes agents and other coding-agent CLIs

Agent Sessions is a local-first macOS application that provides a unified interface for managing and searching history from various AI coding agents. It works by indexing local session folders and databases to allow users to inspect transcripts and browse visual outputs without uploading data to the cloud. The tool supports a wide range of integrations including Hermes, Claude, Cursor, and Codex, and features an Agent Cockpit for monitoring active iTerm2 sessions. Users can recover saved sessions and launch resume commands directly into Terminal, iTerm2, or Warp.

Highlights:
- Unified search and browsing for multiple AI agent histories
- Local-only indexing with no telemetry or remote logging
- Agent Cockpit for live monitoring of active iTerm2 sessions

---

## AaronWong1999/hermesclaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AaronWong1999/hermesclaw
GitHub: https://github.com/AaronWong1999/hermesclaw
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 578
Run Hermes Agent and OpenClaw on the same WeChat account

HermesClaw is an integration bridge that allows Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and OpenCode to operate simultaneously on a single WeChat account. It acts as a central iLink poller and routes messages through local proxy servers to prevent the 403 errors and connection drops caused by multiple gateways competing for the same API token. This architecture enables users to switch between different AI brains or activate multiple agents at once using simple chat commands. It also introduces WeChat support for OpenCode, enabling voice-based coding tasks and access to free models like MiniMax M2.5.

Highlights:
- Runs three AI agents simultaneously on one WeChat account
- Prevents iLink token conflicts via local proxy routing
- Enables agent switching with /hermes, /openclaw, and /opencode commands

---

## Agent-3-7/minions

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Agent-3-7/minions
GitHub: https://github.com/Agent-3-7/minions
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 559
Mission Control for Hermes agents: create, supervise, and review autonomous Hermes Agent work from one screen

Minions is a centralized management interface for Hermes Agent that replaces fragmented terminal sessions with a unified dashboard. It utilizes a Kanban-style board to track persistent agent sessions, allowing users to monitor reasoning, tool calls, and task progress in real time. The system enforces a human-in-the-loop workflow where agents propose completions that require manual verification before being marked as done. Users can also manage scheduled recurring jobs and browse agent-created files through a local-first SQLite architecture.

Highlights:
- Kanban board for tracking autonomous task status and review queues
- Live streaming of agent reasoning and tool execution
- Human-in-the-loop verification for all agent-proposed task completions

Stars: 542
Build autonomous AI agent swarms with orchestration, skills, and multiple model providers

SwarmClaw is an open-source, self-hosted runtime and framework designed for orchestrating autonomous AI agent swarms. It functions as a centralized dashboard where users can manage multi-agent workflows using heartbeats, schedules, and delegation across more than 23 LLM providers. The platform features durable agent memory and a conversation-to-skill learning system to enhance agent capabilities over time. It integrates with various CLI backends and MCP tools, providing a practical environment for complex, delegated task execution.

Highlights:
- Supports 23+ LLM providers including Ollama, OpenAI, and Hermes.
- Orchestrates agent swarms with delegation, schedules, and heartbeats.

## joeynyc/hermes-skins

GitHub: https://github.com/joeynyc/hermes-skins
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 427
Community CLI skins and themes for Hermes terminal UI

Hermes Skins is a collection of visual themes for the Hermes CLI agent that allows users to customize the terminal user interface. It works by utilizing YAML configuration files to override default settings for colors, ASCII art banners, and spinner behaviors. Users can apply these skins for a session or permanently via the config file to change the aesthetic without affecting the agent's behavior. The project includes a wide variety of presets ranging from retro DOS and cyberpunk styles to brand-specific themes.

Highlights:
- Customizes banner colors, ASCII art, and response-box labels
- Supports partial YAML configurations that inherit from default settings
- Includes a diverse library of themed presets and custom skin creation

Opinionated workflow layer for building, shipping, and operating apps with Hermes Agent

Oh My Hermes is an opinionated workflow layer that transforms the Hermes Agent into an autonomous software development and operations engine. It functions as a curated set of 23 skills and 6 specialized agents that manage the entire application lifecycle, from requirement clarification to production deployment. The system orchestrates external tools like Claude Code, Vercel, and Supabase while maintaining persistent memory and a Kanban-based task loop. Users interact with the agent via chat platforms like Telegram or Slack to approve automated pull requests, security scans, and health checks.

Stars: 384
Performance optimization guide for Hermes deployments

The Hermes Optimization Guide is a comprehensive technical resource for deploying and scaling Hermes Agent v0.14.0. It provides 24 instructional parts alongside runnable artifacts like VPS bootstrap scripts, hardened systemd units, and opinionated configuration templates. The guide enables users to implement advanced features such as durable Kanban lanes, MCP server integration, and multi-platform orchestration across 22+ services. It focuses on balancing high-performance agent capabilities with cost-efficiency through model routing and context compression strategies.

## Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory
GitHub: https://github.com/Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 349
Meta-skill plugin that watches workflows and auto-generates reusable skills

Skill Factory is a meta-skill for Hermes Agent that automates the creation of reusable skills by monitoring user workflows. It functions by tracking session patterns in the background and proposing structured skill definitions when it detects repeatable sequences. Users can generate both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds directly from these proposals via slash commands or natural language. This tool streamlines the expansion of the Hermes ecosystem by turning manual environment setups and development tasks into permanent, executable assets.

Highlights:
- Automatically detects and proposes reusable skills from active workflows
- Generates both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds
- Provides slash commands to manage, list, and save detected patterns

---

## Shelpuk-AI-Technology-Consulting/kindly-web-search-mcp-server

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Shelpuk-AI-Technology-Consulting/kindly-web-search-mcp-server
GitHub: https://github.com/Shelpuk-AI-Technology-Consulting/kindly-web-search-mcp-server
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 340
Web search and content retrieval MCP server supporting Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and other AI tools

Kindly Web Search is an MCP server designed to provide AI coding agents with deep content retrieval rather than simple URL snippets. It utilizes a headless browser and direct API integrations to fetch full conversations from platforms like GitHub Issues, StackOverflow, and arXiv in a single call. By delivering structured Markdown content and metadata immediately, it eliminates the need for secondary scraping steps during debugging or research. The tool integrates with agents like Claude Code and Cursor to improve code generation quality by providing up-to-date documentation and community solutions.

Highlights:
- Retrieves full StackOverflow and GitHub conversations in one request
- Uses headless browsers for real-time parsing of any webpage
- Supports Serper, Tavily, and self-hosted SearXNG search providers

---

## DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills
GitHub: https://github.com/DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 304
Type-safe agentskills.io support with progressive disclosure for Pydantic AI

pydantic-ai-skills is a standardized framework for managing modular agent capabilities within the Pydantic AI ecosystem. It implements the Agent Skills open specification by providing a tool-calling mechanism that allows agents to discover and execute specialized scripts or instructions. The library uses a progressive disclosure approach, loading skill details only when relevant to minimize token consumption. Developers can define these skills through local filesystem directories or programmatically using Python decorators and dataclasses.

Highlights:
- Implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI
- Reduces token usage through progressive disclosure of skill metadata
- Supports both filesystem-based and programmatic Python skill definitions

---

## jwangkun/hermes-agent-guide

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/jwangkun/hermes-agent-guide
GitHub: https://github.com/jwangkun/hermes-agent-guide
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 300
A systematic Chinese-language guide to Hermes Agent — 16 books, 300K+ words, covering installation, advanced development, single- and multi-agent orchestration.

The Hermes Agent White Paper is a comprehensive Chinese-language technical guide designed to bridge the documentation gap for the Hermes Agent framework. It provides a structured 16-book curriculum covering the system's five-layer architecture, three-tier memory system, and the integration of over 47 built-in tools. The guide details practical implementations including MCP protocol automation, multi-platform deployment on WeChat and Discord, and migration paths from OpenClaw. Users can follow specialized reading tracks for rapid deployment, deep technical development, or commercial application scenarios.

Highlights:
- 16-book systematic guide with over 300,000 words of technical content
- Deep analysis of five-layer architecture and three-tier memory systems
- Step-by-step integration for 15+ platforms and 6,000+ MCP services

---

## clawshell/clawshell

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/clawshell/clawshell
GitHub: https://github.com/clawshell/clawshell
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 291
The Runtime Security Layer for OpenClaw/Hermes-agent, the essential safety harness for PII & sensitive credentials protection.

ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provides secure email isolation through IMAP sender filtering and supports OAuth-based authentication for providers like OpenAI.

Highlights:
- Maps virtual keys to real credentials stored in protected Unix directories
- Scans and redacts PII in request and response bodies via regex
- Enforces sender-based email filtering for secure mailbox access

---

## elkimek/honcho-self-hosted

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/elkimek/honcho-self-hosted
GitHub: https://github.com/elkimek/honcho-self-hosted
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 286
Self-hosted Honcho memory backend for cross-session persistence

This project provides a configuration layer for self-hosting Plastic Labs' Honcho memory backend, enabling cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent without relying on third-party cloud storage. It deploys the full Honcho stack—including the API, PostgreSQL with pgvector, and Redis—on local hardware using Docker Compose. Users can route memory extraction and reasoning tasks through any OpenAI-compatible API or local inference servers like Ollama to maintain data sovereignty. This setup allows Hermes to build long-term user profiles and logical observations while keeping conversation data on the user's own infrastructure.

Highlights:
- Enables private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent via self-hosting
- Supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider or local inference servers
- Includes automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis

---

## stainlu/hermes-labyrinth

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/stainlu/hermes-labyrinth
GitHub: https://github.com/stainlu/hermes-labyrinth
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 278
Read-only observability plugin for Hermes Agent with journeys, crossings, guideposts, and reports

Hermes Labyrinth is a read-only observability plugin for Hermes Agent that visualizes autonomous workflows as a structured map of events. It functions as a black-box recorder, reading local state to track prompts, tool calls, model transitions, and subagent activities without mutating session data. The tool provides deep diagnostic visibility through specialized views for skill overrides, scheduled cron tasks, and redacted journey reports. Users can export evidence in Markdown or JSON formats while relying on built-in redaction to protect sensitive credentials.

Highlights:
- Visualizes agent journeys through ordered crossings and event maps
- Provides diagnostic views for skill overrides and cron schedules
- Generates redacted Markdown and JSON reports for session evidence

---

## ucsandman/DashClaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ucsandman/DashClaw
GitHub: https://github.com/ucsandman/DashClaw
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 273
Decision infrastructure with guard policies and risk assessment for agents

DashClaw is a governance and decision infrastructure layer designed to intercept and evaluate risky AI agent actions before they execute on real systems. It functions as a middleware that enforces declarative policies, manages human-in-the-loop approvals, and records verifiable evidence for every agent decision. The platform prevents double-execution of tasks through durable outcome tracking and provides cryptographic attribution using OIDC bearer tokens. It integrates with major frameworks including LangChain, CrewAI, and AutoGen, as well as specific agents like Hermes Agent and Claude Code.

Highlights:
- Evaluates and blocks risky agent actions based on declarative policies.
- Routes pending actions to human approval queues via CLI or mobile.
- Provides native lifecycle hooks and plugins for Hermes Agent integration.

---

## raulvidis/hermes-android

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/raulvidis/hermes-android
GitHub: https://github.com/raulvidis/hermes-android
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 252
Android device control — bridge app + Python toolset for mobile automation

hermes-android is a remote control system that enables AI agents to interact with Android devices. It utilizes a Kotlin-based bridge app that connects to a server via WebSockets, allowing the agent to execute commands through the Android AccessibilityService. This architecture bypasses the need for VPNs, USB connections, or port forwarding using a simple pairing code. The system supports a wide range of mobile automation tasks and is also compatible with Android Automotive OS head units.

Highlights:
- 38 tools for screen interaction, app control, and device hardware
- WebSocket-based connection working behind NAT without port forwarding
- Support for Android Automotive OS car head units

---

YouTube Skills is a collection of tools that enables AI agents to interact with YouTube content without relying on browser automation or local binaries. It utilizes the TranscriptAPI backend to bypass cloud IP blocks, providing agents with direct access to video transcripts, search results, and channel metadata. The project integrates with Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and Claude Code using the Agent Skills format. Users can perform tasks like summarizing videos, extracting playlist contents, and searching for specific channel uploads through natural language prompts.

Highlights:
- Extracts transcripts, captions, and timestamps without using yt-dlp
- Supports video search, channel browsing, and playlist extraction
- Compatible with Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and Claude Code

## 335234131/agent-browser-mcp

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/335234131/agent-browser-mcp
GitHub: https://github.com/335234131/agent-browser-mcp
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 223
让 Agent 直接操作真实 Chrome 的 MCP 服务，支持页面扫描、CDP、截图与物理输入

Agent-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that enables AI agents to interact directly with a user's active Chrome browser session rather than a sandboxed environment. It works through a local bridge and a Chrome extension to maintain existing login states, cookies, and open tabs while providing low-level control. The project allows agents to perform page scanning, execute JavaScript, and issue Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) commands. It also supports physical hardware interaction, including real mouse movements, keyboard inputs, and desktop screenshots.

Highlights:
- Operates real Chrome sessions with preserved login states and cookies
- Executes native CDP commands for advanced DOM and browser control
- Supports physical mouse and keyboard inputs for human-like automation

It utilizes the agentskills.io open standard and an automated security scanner that checks for threats like prompt injection and data exfiltration. ## kaminocorp/hermes-alpha

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/kaminocorp/hermes-alpha
GitHub: https://github.com/kaminocorp/hermes-alpha
Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 213
Cloud-deployed Hermes with pre-configured templates and managed infrastructure

Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can bootstrap an autonomous bug bounty system from first principles. It utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent Overseer agent manages the codebase, deployment, and optimization of an ephemeral Hunter agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. The system operates via a FastAPI-powered web terminal and leverages Elephantasm for memory streams to drive a self-improvement cycle. This approach tests whether an agent can evolve its own skills and tools without pre-built scaffolding.

Highlights:
- Self-improving loop where Overseer evolves Hunter's code and strategy
- Web terminal interface using FastAPI, WebSockets, and xterm.js
- Deployment on Fly.io with Elephantasm memory and messaging sidecars

## robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus

GitHub: https://github.com/robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 209
Multi-provider web search (Serper, Tavily, Exa, Querit, Perplexity) with auto-routing

This plugin provides multi-provider web search and URL extraction capabilities for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It utilizes a benchmarked auto-routing engine that dynamically selects the best available provider based on the user's configured API keys and query requirements. The tool supports a wide range of backends including Serper, Tavily, Exa, and Linkup, ensuring search reliability through conservative fallback paths and quality diagnostics. Users can manage costs and performance through a dedicated setup wizard that configures provider priorities and extraction preferences.

Highlights:
- Adds routed multi-provider web search and clean URL extraction tools.
- Features Routing v2 for class-aware provider selection and fallback.
- Supports over twelve providers including You.com, Firecrawl, and Perplexity.

## Cranot/super-hermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Cranot/super-hermes
GitHub: https://github.com/Cranot/super-hermes
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 209
Skills that teach Hermes Agent to write its own analytical prompts

Super Hermes is a skill registry for the Hermes Agent that enables models to generate their own analytical frameworks, or prisms, for deep code and document inspection. It works by instructing the agent to write specific thinking instructions before execution, allowing it to derive structural trade-offs and conservation laws rather than just identifying surface-level bugs. The system includes specialized commands like /prism-reflect, which provide a constraint report detailing what the analysis missed to ensure transparency in the agent's cognitive limits.

Highlights:
- Teaches agents to write custom thinking instructions for specific problems
- Identifies structural conservation laws and trade-offs in complex codebases
- Generates constraint reports detailing analysis blind spots and limitations

---

Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 187
Hermes with integrated CaMeL trust boundaries for safer autonomous execution

This project is a self-improving AI agent designed for autonomous execution with integrated safety boundaries. It utilizes a closed learning loop to create and refine skills from experience while employing a CaMeL-style runtime guard to prevent indirect prompt injections. The agent supports a wide array of LLM providers and can be deployed across various environments, including serverless infrastructure and cloud VMs. Users can interact with the agent via a dedicated terminal interface or through messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, and Slack.

Highlights:
- Self-improving learning loop for autonomous skill creation and refinement
- CaMeL-style runtime guard to block unauthorized sensitive tool side effects
- Multi-platform access via CLI and various messaging gateway integrations

## ReinaMacCredy/maestro

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ReinaMacCredy/maestro
GitHub: https://github.com/ReinaMacCredy/maestro
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 184
Harness for long-running AI agents — structured memory, cross-feature learning, plan-approve-execute

Maestro is a Rust-based, local-first harness designed to provide a durable execution environment for coding agents. It manages the agent lifecycle by storing features, tasks, and decision logs as plain files within a repository's `.maestro/` directory. The system enforces gated state transitions, requiring agents to provide verifiable proof of work before tasks can be marked as complete. This approach ensures that agent actions remain auditable, persistent across sessions, and integrated directly into standard version control workflows.

Highlights:
- Local-first architecture using plain files without background daemons
- Gated state machine for features, tasks, and harness improvements
- Evidence-based verification requiring recorded proof for task completion

---

## Signet-AI/signetai

GitHub: https://github.com/Signet-AI/signetai
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 182
Local-first identity, memory, and secrets layer for AI agents — portable state across Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, and MCP clients

Signet-AI is a local-first context and memory layer designed to provide persistent identity and state for AI agents across different platforms. It functions as a durable infrastructure layer using SQLite and local workspace files to store transcripts, source records, and secrets. Users can inspect and correct agent memories through a dedicated dashboard and command-line interface to ensure recall accuracy. The system integrates directly with various harnesses including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and MCP clients to maintain portable context when switching tools.

Highlights:
- Local-first storage for memories, identity, and secrets using SQLite
- Repairable memory system for editing, deleting, or reclassifying agent context
- Portable state across Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and MCP clients

## yoloshii/ClawMem

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/yoloshii/ClawMem
GitHub: https://github.com/yoloshii/ClawMem
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 180
On-device memory and context engine for agents — local-first, no cloud dependencies

ClawMem is a local-first memory and context engine that provides AI agents with persistent, on-device knowledge. It utilizes a hybrid architecture combining multi-signal retrieval, intent classification, and multi-graph traversal to surface relevant context from markdown notes and session transcripts. The system integrates with Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes agents via a shared SQLite vault. It enables agents to capture decisions, generate session handoffs, and maintain a self-evolving memory layer without cloud dependencies.

Highlights:
- Local-first architecture with no API keys or cloud dependencies
- Hybrid retrieval using BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs
- Integrates via MCP server, Claude Code hooks, and agent plugins

## 42-evey/hermes-plugins

Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 170
Goal management, inter-agent bridge, model selection, and cost control plugins

Evey's Hermes Plugins is a collection of 23 custom extensions designed to enhance the autonomy, observability, and cost-efficiency of the Hermes Agent framework. The suite implements specialized logic for multi-model decision debates, smart task routing with fallback chains, and structured telemetry logging. These tools enable agents to manage their own goals, perform self-correction loops, and consolidate long-term memory into vector storage. Additionally, the project includes a bidirectional bridge for Claude Code and budget enforcement via Langfuse integration.

- Autonomous planning, reflection, and multi-model consensus debate tools
- Smart model routing with sensitivity filters and four-model fallback chains
- Automated memory consolidation into Qdrant vectors and identity evolution

## tlehman/litprog-skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/tlehman/litprog-skill
GitHub: https://github.com/tlehman/litprog-skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 167
Literate programming skill — weave code and documentation across agents

The litprog-skill is a Claude Code extension that implements Donald Knuth's literate programming paradigm by transforming codebases into human-readable narratives. It works by generating a .lit.md file that weaves together prose, Mermaid diagrams, and LaTeX math with the original source code. The tool includes a tangler to extract runnable files and a reverse-sync engine to ensure the documentation stays updated when source files are edited. This approach helps developers document architectural decisions and facilitates easier onboarding through structured, essay-like code explanations.

Highlights:
- Generates .lit.md files combining prose, diagrams, and source code
- Extracts runnable source files from documentation using a tangler
- Automatically syncs source file edits back to the literate document

---

Portable Hermes Agent is a Windows-native desktop application that provides a user-friendly interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring Docker or administrative rights. It operates through a Tkinter-based GUI or CLI, utilizing an embedded Python environment to connect with local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The agent features a library of 100 tools for tasks including music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation. Users can also build custom automation pipelines through a built-in workflow engine and a dynamic tool maker for wrapping REST APIs.

Highlights:

- Dynamic tool maker and workflow engine for custom automation pipelines

Portable Hermes Agent is a standalone Windows desktop application that provides a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring system-wide installations or administrative rights. It operates by bundling an embedded Python environment and connecting to local models via LM Studio or cloud providers through an OpenAI-compatible API. The platform features over 100 tools for tasks such as music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation, alongside a workflow engine for multi-step automation. Users can also extend the agent's capabilities at runtime using a built-in dynamic tool maker for API wrapping and Python scripting.

## plur-ai/plur

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/plur-ai/plur
GitHub: https://github.com/plur-ai/plur
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 153
Shared memory layer with open engram YAML format for multi-agent systems

PLUR is a persistent, local-first memory layer designed to synchronize agent corrections, preferences, and conventions across different tools and sessions. It stores knowledge as plain YAML "engrams" on disk and uses a hybrid BM25 and embedding-based retrieval system to inject relevant context into agent prompts. The system employs an ACT-R activation model where facts strengthen with use and naturally decay over time to prevent context clutter. It integrates directly with MCP-compatible tools, Claude Code, and Hermes Agent to automate the capture and recall of project-specific knowledge.

Highlights:
- Stores memory locally as open-format YAML engrams with zero API costs
- Uses ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale ones
- Provides MCP tools for automated learning and hybrid context injection

---

Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web dashboard designed to serve as the central command center for self-hosted Hermes Agents. The interface enables users to manage chat sessions, browse workspace files, monitor real-time logs, and schedule cron jobs. Learn Hermes Agent is a 27-chapter hands-on tutorial for building production-grade autonomous AI agents from scratch using Python. The curriculum guides developers through implementing a core agent loop, tool registration, session persistence with SQLite, and context compression. It covers advanced infrastructure including multi-platform gateways for Telegram and Discord, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, and RL-based self-evolution. The project provides runnable reference implementations for each stage to help users understand the design backbone of autonomous systems.

Highlights:
- Step-by-step implementation of agent loops and tool dispatch systems
- Multi-platform gateway supporting Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WeChat

## linke-ai/hermes-agent-team

Local multi-agent team collaboration web system built on Hermes Agent profiles, MCP, ACP, and Hermes Kanban

Hermes Agents Team is a local web-based system designed to orchestrate multi-agent collaboration using Hermes Agent profiles. It utilizes a Leader/Specialist architecture to automate task decomposition, assignment, and review through a centralized Web UI and Hermes Kanban. The system integrates MCP and ACP protocols to manage agent communications, skills, and tool calls entirely within a local environment. Users can monitor real-time dialogue, terminal outputs, and task progression while maintaining full control over model configurations and data privacy.

- Integrates MCP and ACP protocols for local tool and communication handling

## esaradev/icarus-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/esaradev/icarus-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/esaradev/icarus-plugin
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 128
Self-memory and replacement models — remember your work, train your replacement

Icarus is a Hermes plugin designed to create self-improving agents through shared memory and automated model fine-tuning. It captures agent decisions, session outcomes, and structured notes into a markdown-based "fabric" directory that can be optionally managed via Obsidian. This data is then used to extract high-quality training pairs for fine-tuning cheaper replacement models via Together AI. The system includes a full pipeline for evaluating candidate models against base versions and switching to them once performance thresholds are met.

Highlights:
- Cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories
- Automated training data extraction with decision-quality tagging
- End-to-end pipeline for fine-tuning and evaluating replacement models

---

## 1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent

GitHub: https://github.com/1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 124
17 specialized agents for research, planning, implementation, quality, and infrastructure

Hermes is a multi-agent orchestration system for OpenCode AI that automates complex software development workflows. It utilizes a master orchestrator to route requests through specialized pipelines consisting of 17 distinct AI agents. The system ensures software reliability by enforcing mandatory quality gates and security audits before deployment. It supports diverse use cases including feature development, bug fixing, and performance optimization through structured context passing between agents.

Highlights:
- 17 specialized agents for research, planning, and implementation
- Mandatory quality gates via reviewer and tester agents
- Customizable pipelines for security, refactoring, and infrastructure changes

---

## AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow
GitHub: https://github.com/AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 124
Pre-build idea-to-spec workflow skills for Hermes Agent that turn rough ideas into design docs and implementation handoffs

The Hermes Agent Idea Workflow is a suite of skills designed to transform vague product concepts into structured, build-ready specifications. It operates through a staged pipeline that separates product intent from technical implementation, utilizing guided interviews and a reusable question bank to generate markdown artifacts. The system supports both a Lite mode for quick capture and a Full mode that produces comprehensive design docs, UI briefs, and implementation specs. These outputs are specifically formatted to serve as direct handoff material for downstream coding workflows like Superpowers for GPT.

Highlights:
- Converts rough ideas into structured design and implementation documents
- Features Lite and Full modes for varying project complexity
- Includes a spec review gate with PASS/FAIL evaluation models

---

## EfficientContext/ContextPilot

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/EfficientContext/ContextPilot
GitHub: https://github.com/EfficientContext/ContextPilot
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 113
Accelerating Long Context LLM Inference with Accuracy-Preserving Context Optimization in SGLang, vLLM, llama.cpp, OpenClaw, RAG, and Agentic AI.

ContextPilot is a context optimization engine designed to accelerate long-context LLM inference by maximizing KV cache reuse. It functions by maintaining a context index that reorders and deduplicates overlapping context blocks into shared prefixes before they reach the inference server. This approach reduces redundant token computation and prefill latency without compromising model reasoning quality. The project provides native integrations for Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, vLLM, and SGLang, supporting both local deployments and cloud APIs.

Highlights:
- Increases cache hits by 4–12× through prefix reordering and deduplication
- Reduces prefill latency by 1.5–3× across RAG and agentic workloads
- Native plugin support for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw ecosystems

---

## AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC
GitHub: https://github.com/AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 111
Hermes Agent ported to Claude Code Channel — 46 native skills, no OAuth, no external process

hermes-CCC is a port of the Hermes Agent framework specifically designed for the Claude Code native skill system. It integrates the Hermes brain architecture directly into the Claude Code execution context, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth authentication. The project implements 46 core skills across categories such as MLOps, software development, and research, including specialized tools for RL training and vector database management. This allows developers to utilize Hermes-style memory, personas, and task routing while remaining within a unified terminal environment.

Highlights:
- Port of Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills to CCC
- Runs natively in Claude Code without separate processes or OAuth
- Includes MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and RL training

---

## smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills
